compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can use DSO skills via wrapper commands when the marketplace plugin is not installed
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Create .claude/commands/ wrapper files for key DSO skills that use shell preprocessing to detect marketplace plugin installation and either delegate to the plugin skill or read from local plugins/dso/skills/.
**Why**: In the Claude Code web environment (and any environment without the marketplace plugin), developers need access to DSO skills. Wrapper commands provide zero-config access via committed markdown files that work everywhere.
**Scope**:
- IN: Create wrapper commands for user-invocable skills (brainstorm, sprint, fix-bug, debug-everything, preplanning, implementation-plan, end-session, tdd-workflow, review, commit); shell preprocessing detection via claude plugin list; graceful degradation on detection failure; wrapper-skill parity test
- OUT: Creating wrappers for non-user-invocable skills, agent definitions, or hooks — those are covered by the CLAUDE.md agent fallback directive (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a developer without the marketplace plugin installed can invoke wrapper commands (e.g., /brainstorm) and the command reads and executes from the local plugins/dso/skills/ files
  ← Satisfies: "A developer opens this repo in Claude Code for the web... can invoke wrapper commands"
- When this story is complete, a developer with the marketplace plugin installed invokes the same wrapper command and it delegates to the plugin skill (e.g., /dso:brainstorm) via the Skill tool
  ← Satisfies: "When the DSO marketplace plugin is installed, wrapper commands delegate to the installed plugin skills"
- When this story is complete, if claude plugin list is unavailable or errors, the wrapper gracefully falls back to reading local skill files without user-visible errors
  ← Satisfies: "If detection fails or times out, graceful degradation to local fallback"
- When this story is complete, an automated test verifies that every user-invocable skill in plugins/dso/skills/ has a corresponding .claude/commands/ wrapper file
  ← Satisfies: scenario analysis "Wrapper-skill parity not enforced"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for wrapper detection logic and fallback behavior
  ← Satisfies: unit test DoD requirement

## Considerations

- [Reliability] claude plugin list may be unavailable in web/CI — detection must degrade gracefully to local fallback
- [Maintainability] Wrapper-skill parity must be enforced — new skills without wrappers leave web users without access
- [Performance] Shell preprocessing adds invocation latency — include timeout on detection command
- [Scenario] Skill tool invocation may fail on false-positive detection — wrapper instructions should include fallback to local SKILL.md if Skill tool dispatch fails
